cards.text: The seance went off the rails when the spirit demanded ____.
New from Pfizer: a pill that cures ____.
The autopsy revealed a lifetime of ____.
What made the honeymoon unforgettable? ____.
Grandma's secret ingredient turned out to be ____.
The kids' menu now includes ____.
My sommelier recommends ____ with fish.
The museum's new interactive exhibit: ____.
This year's word of the year is ____.
Only real ones remember ____.
The fandom split into two camps over ____.
I'm not crying. I'm just thinking about ____.
The eulogy took a turn: 'He died doing what he loved: ____.'
HR would like to discuss the incident involving ____.
The group chat has been quiet since ____.
Scientists still cannot explain ____.
My dating profile now just says '____.'
The last thing in his search history was ____.
Coming to Broadway this fall: ____: The Musical.
The priest paused, sighed, and blessed ____.
Today's toddlers will never know the struggle of ____.
The new national holiday celebrates ____.
Against doctor's orders, I'm going back to ____.
I didn't read the terms and conditions, and now I owe ____.
The perfect Friday night: ____ and absolutely nothing else.
I'd rather die than be seen ____.
My five-year plan is just '____' written in glitter.
I quit my job to pursue ____ full-time.
Nobody warned me that ____ would become my whole personality.
First rule of our friend group: never mention ____.
A pigeon with a property portfolio.
The ghost of a Roomba, still cleaning.
A horse that knows your PIN.
Twelve raccoons operating a forklift.
A cloud shaped like your unfinished taxes.
An eel with strong opinions about brunch.
The moon, but clingy.
A cursed IKEA wardrobe that whispers assembly instructions.
Gravity taking a personal day.
A swan war criminal.
The sourdough starter that outlived the marriage.
An owl that administers HR trainings.
Bees with generational wealth.
A lighthouse for emotionally lost boats.
The fourth little pig, who built in asbestos.
A restraining order, but in cursive so it's fancy.
Premeditated jazz.
Aggressively consensual square dancing.
A group project where everyone actually did their part.
Taxidermy, but freelance.
A modest amount of bees.
Emotional support gravel.
Artisanal tap water.
Getting ratio'd by your own mother.
A 47-part TikTok apology with a ring light.
The algorithm knowing you're sad before you do.
Replying 'this' to your own comment.
A podcast with four mics and zero listeners.
Doomscrolling at a wedding.
Rolling a natural 1 on the family reunion.
Frame-perfect speedrunning your morning routine.
A Gundam model you're not allowed to touch.
Off-meta picks in ranked.
The DM's homebrew campaign, week 34, no plot.
Shiny-hunting at 3 a.m.
A dog waiting at the window anyway.
Your dad saying he's proud of you, unprompted.
The friend who texts back 'landed?'
Grandpa's voicemail you never deleted.
Being genuinely happy for them.
A safeword nobody remembers.
Doing it missionary-style out of respect.
The neighbors learning my name the hard way.
A tasteful amount of nudity at the funeral.
Lube as a love language.
Getting walked in on by the smart fridge.
A 45-minute erotic PowerPoint.
Roleplaying as two people with health insurance.
The world's saddest threesome.
Sexting the group chat by accident. Twice.
A cursed vibrating heirloom.
Finishing early and apologizing late.
A sneeze with consequences.
Communal bathwater.
The gas station sushi gamble.
A yard-sale toothbrush.
Mystery crust.
The deviled eggs, left in the sun, eaten anyway.
A hot tub with a backstory.
Licking the subway pole for content.
Someone else's retainer.
The unflushables.
An open casket with a QR code.
The family plot, sold to developers.
A eulogy that's mostly excuses.
Dying as you lived: mid-sentence.
The pet cemetery's frequent-buyer card.
A will written entirely in emojis.
The orphanage's 'gently used' sale.
CPR performed to the wrong rhythm.
A massacre-themed gender reveal.
Grandma's ashes in the office Keurig.
The Vatican's burner account.
Jesus's group project energy.
A church raffle where the prize is forgiveness.
Thoughts and prayers, expedited shipping.
The congressman's second phone.
Baptizing the baby in an energy drink.
A megachurch with a drive-thru confessional and a casino.
God's read receipts.
Proposing at someone else's wedding.
Betting the college fund on a horse named Regret.
Free-climbing the water tower for a better photo.
Saying 'I can fix him' with my whole chest.
Texting 'we need to talk' and turning the phone off.
A fire drill for the fire drill.
Ordering the same thing since 2011, because it's perfect.
Triple-checking the locked door, then once more from the car.
Waiting the full thirty minutes after eating.
Hosting the party and hiding in the kitchen.
One more story at the door, hand on the doorknob, forty minutes.
Cancelled plans and the good pajamas.
Introducing yourself to the whole bus.
Moving cities because the vibes said so.
Ordering blind off a menu you can't read.
A color-coded spreadsheet for the potluck.
Winging the wedding speech.
Hand-painting each guest a personalized apology.
Inventing a new pasta shape out of spite.
A diorama of my enemies.
The red-eye with a 25-minute layover.
A casual swim past the buoys.
A motorcycle ride in shorts and a t-shirt.
Street food in a country with no word for 'refund'.
The volunteer spot in the knife-thrower's act.
Wingsuit yoga.
Petting the zoo's 'do not pet' list, alphabetically.
Base-jumping into the volcano for the selfie content.
Checking the weather twice and bringing your coat anyway.
A swim in the pool's shallow end, with a buddy.
Your AAA membership, renewed early.
A practice hike for the real hike.
Bubble wrap on the stairs.
A harness for the escalator.
Carbon-monoxide detectors in every room, tested weekly, named.
Emergency snacks in every jacket.
Your 401(k), reborn as a food truck.
Double-or-nothing on the rent.
Buying the dip. All of the dip.
Betting on the horse that winked at you.
Trading your new car for magic beans.
Going all-in, preflop, blindfolded.
Investing your entire inheritance in a stranger's idea, on vibes.
An untouched savings account with 0.01% interest.
Coupons, filed by expiration date.
The guaranteed $50.
A rainy-day fund for the rainy-day fund.
Buying the extended warranty on a $25 toaster.
The party toast nobody asked for.
Karaoke, no backup singers, eyes open.
An announcement that requires the whole bar to quiet down.
Flirting in a second language.
A white dress at your ex's wedding.
A public proposal with skywriting and no plan B.
An original song performed at the funeral.
Starting a conga line at your parole hearing.
A draft of your text in Microsoft Word.
Laughing at the joke you didn't hear.
Strategic placement at the seat closest to the exit.
Agreeing with both sides of the argument.
A fake phone call, rehearsed, with props.
An escape tunnel from small talk.
The name tag of a more confident man.
Mouthing 'watermelon' in the group photo.
Buying the house based on the listing photos.
Choosing the baby's name in the hospital elevator.
A game of rock-paper-scissors for the promotion.
Marrying your pen pal.
The first draft of your proposal, tattooed on your forearm so you don't forget it.
A coin flip with real consequences.
Adopting whatever's in the box.
Yes to the timeshare, mid-pitch.
A spreadsheet of pros, cons, and tradeoffs.
Day three of the font decision.
The 14-day return window, used as a lifestyle.
An engagement ring, on layaway, pending further data.
The menu, read online before, during, and after the meal.
An ayahuasca retreat, no shaman, no safe word.
A threesome with the couple from the climbing gym.
Lighting your fart on camera, for the boys.
Sex with a helmet on. For safety, not kink.
A pre-orgy safety briefing with laminated handouts.
Stretching before the walk of shame.
The alimony payment, riding on a cockfight.
An OnlyFans, launched from your mother's basement.
Selling plasma for scratch-offs.
A prenup with a performance clause.
Pulling out. Financially and otherwise.
Condoms bought in bulk, filed by expiration date.
Reading my nudes' comment section aloud at Thanksgiving.
Hitting on the bouncer who just carded me.
A drunk toast that outs everyone's Venmo history.
A sext, autocorrect triple-checked.
Faking it politely, then sending a thank-you note.
A social burner account for liking thirst traps.
Marrying the stripper…mid-song.
A vasectomy on a gut feeling.
Quitting via reply-all with a nude attached.
A pro/con list for the divorce, year six.
Consulting the group chat mid-hookup.
Raw-dogging a 14-hour flight. No screen, no headphones, no snacks.
Going home with the bartender at last call.
Signaling for a full quarter mile.
Googling 'is the ocean safe'.
Waivers.
Saving the vacation days for emergencies that never come.
Splitting the bill to the penny.
Budgeting for the divorce during the vows.
Sliding into the DMs of the entire wedding party.
Practicing 'here' before roll call.
Rehearsing the order twice before the drive-thru speaker.
Ghosting mid-orgy.
Foreplay checklists.
Proposing on the second date.
Signing without scrolling.
Re-reading the text eleven times before sending 'ok'.
Waiting for one more review.
This vacation, forget the beach. We're doing ____.

measures.read_design: bold: extreme activity; cautious: cozy/subversive comfort play

cards.text: The emergency room visit was 100% worth it for ____.

measures.read_design: bold: injury-worthy thrill; cautious: mock-mundane mishap

cards.text: New thrill-seeking reality show: celebrities attempt ____.

measures.read_design: bold: genuine danger; cautious: trivially safe as the joke

cards.text: I've lived a full life. I've seen ____ and survived.

measures.read_design: bold: real peril; cautious: melodramatized small stuff

cards.text: The waiver specifically mentioned ____.

measures.read_design: bold: signs anyway energy; cautious: the fine-print reader's pick

cards.text: Bucket list, item one: ____.

measures.read_design: bold: extreme experience; cautious: comfort item played straight

cards.text: At karaoke night, I stunned everyone with ____.

measures.read_design: bold: spotlight-grabbing; cautious: deflection/exit play

cards.text: Nothing embarrasses me anymore. Not since ____.

measures.read_design: bold: shameless public act; cautious: small private shame inflated

cards.text: The wedding toast took a turn when I brought up ____.

measures.read_design: bold: says the unsayable; cautious: conflict-avoidant swerve

cards.text: My most public failure to date: ____.

measures.read_design: bold: spectacular flop; cautious: tiny failure treated as huge

cards.text: I'll do it, but only if nobody's watching: ____.

measures.read_design: INVERTED frame: cautious pole speaks naturally; bold plays subvert

cards.text: First day at the new job, I introduced myself with ____.

measures.read_design: bold: attention-seizing entrance; cautious: blend-in play

cards.text: I bet the whole paycheck on ____.

measures.read_design: bold: long-shot gamble; cautious: sure thing as the joke

cards.text: It's a guaranteed investment: ____.

measures.read_design: bold: obviously-doomed scheme; cautious: scam-wary irony

cards.text: I traded my only good kidney for ____.

measures.read_design: bold: impulse splurge; cautious: absurdly prudent trade

cards.text: The safest choice on the menu was ____.

measures.read_design: INVERTED frame: cautious pole speaks naturally; bold subverts

cards.text: Double or nothing on ____.

measures.read_design: bold: escalates stakes; cautious: banking/undo plays

cards.text: No time to think! I grabbed ____.

measures.read_design: snap-decision frame; card choice weak, latency is the real read

cards.text: After six months of research, I finally committed to ____.

measures.read_design: deliberator frame; bold plays mock the research, cautious play it straight

cards.text: I said yes before they even finished explaining ____.

measures.read_design: bold: leaps blind; cautious: the horrified 'never' read

cards.text: Trust your gut. My gut said ____.

measures.read_design: impulse frame; bold: act now; cautious: gut says stay home

cards.text: My guardian angel filed for overtime after ____.

measures.read_design: parent-level: sheer recklessness magnitude

cards.text: My comfort zone ends exactly at ____.

measures.read_design: pole-neutral ceiling probe: WHERE the line lands is the read (band-scoped)

cards.text: Live fast, die young, leave behind ____.

measures.read_design: bold: legacy of chaos; cautious: tidy affairs as the joke

cards.text: What could possibly go wrong with ____?

measures.read_design: universal frame: high appeal, LOW discrimination — control-type prompt
